note: Main courtyards are paved and largely step-free; free loaner manual wheelchairs at the cloakroom near the entrance, low threshold at Wat Phra Kaew. No handrails on some steps and open courtyards have brutal sun/heat - hard for seniors. Dress code enforced.

Temple of the Reclining Buddha, a short level walk from the Grand Palace; ramps throughout much of the property make it fairly accessible (though a few reports note tricky spots). Heat is the main challenge.

Temple of Dawn across the river; the ground-level terraces and riverside plaza are paved and step-free, but the central prang has very steep external staircases and is NOT navigable in a wheelchair. Reached by a cross-river ferry (pier access varies).

Chao Phraya Express runs accessible boats with a wide boarding ramp and ground/onboard assistants; a scenic accessible way to see riverside sights. Pier ramps vary in quality - easiest to stay aboard as a sightseeing loop.

The world's largest weekend market (15,000+ stalls); because it lines streets some sections are rollable, but many areas lack curb cuts, keep internal roads open, and get extremely congested (150k+ visitors). Patience and picking accessible sections is key.

Vibrant food/street-art district; Talat Noi's streets are smooth and largely pedestrian and roll well, but the main Chinatown sidewalks are packed with vendors/people and many shops have a step. Great atmosphere, slow going.
Central green park with wide flat paved paths; a relaxed, accessible escape from the city heat and traffic. Good for families and a rest.
Legendary backpacker street; the level street itself is rollable and lively with street food and markets, though crowded and chaotic. Some venues have steps.
Traditional teak-house museum of Thai silk; historic wooden architecture means steps and raised floors limit full access - grounds/ground level partly manageable. Confirm on arrival.
Modern air-conditioned riverside malls; fully accessible with lifts, smooth floors, accessible restrooms and river views. A cool, easy, family-friendly break from the heat.
